To:      AP Team <ap@tamween.sa>
CC:      Reem Al-Harbi <reem.alharbi@tamween.sa>
Subject: HOLD — Najd Foodstuff invoice INV-NF-2026-1184 (disputed)
Date:    Thursday, 19 March 2026 11:07 +03:00

AP team,

Please put a HOLD on payment for Najd Foodstuff invoice INV-NF-2026-1184
(SAR 88,420). The vendor has invoiced us for 1,200 cartons of date paste
delivered to the Dammam DC on 11 March, but our WH receiving log only
confirms 1,040 cartons received in good condition — 160 cartons arrived
broken and were rejected at the gate.

I've asked Najd's account manager (Bashir Khalifa, copied below) to
re-issue the invoice at SAR 76,628 (1,040 × 73.68 unit) plus VAT, less the
SAR 11,792 short-shipment credit. Until the corrected invoice lands, no
payment should be released against the original.

Najd's response will likely take 7–10 days based on prior turnaround.
I'll loop back in once we have the corrected paperwork.

This is a routine dispute — nothing systemic. We've had two similar issues
with Najd in the last 18 months and they've always settled cleanly.

Thanks,

Ahmad Al-Qahtani
Procurement Specialist — Eastern Province
Tamween Distribution Co.
M: +966-50-887-2231

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Bashir Khalifa <accounts@najdfoodstuff.sa>
To:   Ahmad Al-Qahtani <ahmad.alqahtani@tamween.sa>
Subject: RE: Short-shipment Dammam DC 11-Mar — INV-NF-2026-1184

Mr. Al-Qahtani,

Acknowledged. We will inspect the rejected stock at our Dammam transit
warehouse and issue a corrected invoice plus credit note by end of next
week. Apologies for the inconvenience.

Regards,
Bashir Khalifa — Najd Foodstuff
